steps.title: Identify the key assumption
steps.content: The passage cites Zheng claiming “world music” “homogenizes highly distinct traditions” by lumping them together. For this criticism to hold, mbaqanga and quan họ must actually be distinct from each other—not similar.

steps.title: Test which choice proves distinctness
steps.content: Choice A demonstrates they “developed independently of each other and have little in common musically”—confirming these are genuinely distinct traditions. Choices B, C, and D address popularity, performance context, or claim the traditions are similar to each other; none establish distinctness. That matches choice A — A is correct.
